---
title: "Leons_Script"
author: "Leon Hofmann"
date: "2026-03-04"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r}
library(CspStandSegmentation)
```

```{r}
cloudDir <- "/Users/leonhofmann/Desktop/MMES/Protject 2/data/3dt_404/data/raw/3dtree_404_3596_raw.laz"

#load data
las <- lidR::readTLS(cloudDir)

```

```{r}


#plot data (not on mac)

#classify ground
las<- lidR::classify_ground(las,lidR::csf())
head(las@data$X)


# DTM generation
dtm <- las |> lidR::rasterize_terrain(res = 0.1, algorithm = lidR::tin())
terra::plot(dtm)

```

```{r}
install.packages("CspStandSegmentation")


dsm <- las |> lidR::rasterize_canopy(res = 0.1, algorithm = lidR::pitfree())
terra::plot(dsm)

chm <- dsm-dtm
terra::plot(chm)

las_norm <- las - dtm


#find tree positions
map <- las_norm |> CspStandSegmentation::find_base_coordinates_raster(eps=1)


las_seg <- las_norm |> CspStandSegmentation::csp_cost_segmentation(map)

lidR::plot(las_seg, color = "TreeID")

#run forest inventory
inventory <- CspStandSegmentation::forest_inventory(las_seg)


```

## Process Data

```{r,}
#hier ist der code

```

# Analysis



ehehhehe
```{r}


```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars, echo = FALSE}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
